原文:

André N. Darcie

Posted on May 30

   

Antes de tudo, é importante ressaltar que, embora seja incrível ter o hobby de jogar videogame, isso pode levar a um estilo de vida sedentário. Portanto, é importante praticar atividades físicas diárias, ter uma boa qualidade de sono e evitar o vício em jogos, limitando o tempo de jogo a duas horas por dia. Dito isso, vamos discutir um dos hobbies mais divertidos de todos os tempos e como aprimorá-lo.

Evitando ficar o dia todo no mesmo ambiente

Como um desenvolvedor de software, sou parte do público-alvo deste site. Nos dias de hoje, é comum trabalhar em casa, e muitas pessoas desejam continuar nesse modelo. No entanto, passar quase o dia inteiro no mesmo lugar pode ser como estar preso sem ter cometido um crime. Embora você possa se acostumar, isso pode ser prejudicial para a mente e o corpo. Para melhorar minha qualidade de vida, decidi adotar uma abordagem que nunca pensei que tomaria: comprar uma TV e um console. Agora, meu ambiente de lazer, onde jogo videogame, é completamente separado do ambiente do computador e do escritório. Isso não só melhorou significativamente minha qualidade de vida, mas também me permitiu focar melhor em ambas as atividades.

Evitando distrações

Quando eu jogava no PC, não conseguia passar muito tempo sem me distrair. Ficava pressionando Alt+Tab e começava a ler redes sociais, notícias e navegar pela internet por horas sem perceber. Tudo o que eu queria era jogar um pouco, distrair a mente e descansar mentalmente, mas de alguma forma eu sempre era puxado para fora do jogo para fazer outras coisas no PC. O Nintendo Switch não possui acesso a redes sociais nem à internet livremente, e isso é uma "feature", não um problema. Isso me ajuda a focar completamente no que estou fazendo, que, no caso, é jogar.

Experiência de usuário

Embora eu trabalhe na área de Tecnologia da Informação, às vezes é cansativo ter que configurar os jogos para rodarem corretamente no meu computador. Nem todos os jogos são otimizados para todos os tipos de computadores possíveis, então sempre tenho que fazer ajustes finos, testar a taxa de quadros por segundo (FPS), desativar recursos, alterar a resolução e até pensar em adquirir peças melhores ou fazer um upgrade de memória. Com o console, é só ligá-lo, colocar o jogo e começar a jogar, sem preocupações, dores de cabeça ou configurações complicadas. Atualmente, possuo o PlayStation 5 e não me preocupo em gastar com novas peças para ele. A única coisa é que daqui a seis anos será lançado o PlayStation 6, mas até lá minha mente estará tranquila.

Lazer de qualidade

Atualmente, cada vez mais pessoas estão viciadas em seus celulares, passando horas distraídas vendo vídeos. Eu no final do dia, antes de dormir, depois de passar horas navegando a internet no celular, me pego pensando: "nada disso valeu a pena". Se eu tivesse me dedicado apenas a jogar algum jogo, teria experimentado algo muito mais divertido e interessante, verdadeiramente distraído minha mente e recarregado minhas energias.

Conclusão

Embora possa parecer um detalhe, ter um console fez uma diferença drástica na minha vida, levando em consideração que meu hobby principal é jogar e que trabalho em casa. Essa mudança proporcionou uma melhoria significativa no meu bem-estar e na qualidade do meu tempo de lazer.

Sloan the DEV Moderator - May 12

Items.Title: 程序员，小心！ 设计师出击！ | Programmers, beware! Designers attack!
Items.Description:
强制性的（我的编辑让我添加它）免责声明😉
本文中出现的所有场景纯属出于幽默目的，并反映作者的个人观点。 目的不是要冒犯任何人或专门针对真实情况或项目。 与实际事件或项目的任何相似之处纯属巧合。

介绍

当您遇到一种设计时，您是否熟悉这种感觉，它会立即在您的脑海中引发一连串的咒骂，让您质疑将您带到那个特定时刻的每一个决定？ 不？ 好吧，认为自己很幸运。 某些设计实践具有引起前端开发人员此类反应的不可思议的能力。 遗憾的是，在项目中遇到这些设计挑战之一通常意味着很可能会遇到更多挑战。 在本文中，我们将深入研究在开发过程中造成重大障碍的设计领域。 做好准备，踏上一段深入了解令人沮丧的设计选择世界的旅程。 让我们一起踏上这场冒险吧！

对角线

让我们深入探讨许多 Web 开发人员面临的一个常见困境，无论他们的经验水平如何。 如果幸运的话，您可以挤出一些圆角，但除此之外的任何事情都需要深入研究 hacky 解决方案领域。 而这正是我们要探索的。 想象一下：一位好心的设计师决定在网站的不同部分之间使用“动态”和“有趣”的分隔线来增加趣味性。

有很多方法可以解决这个问题，但我个人更喜欢使用 SVG 三角形，如下图所示。 实施这个解决方案并不过分复杂，但在缩放浏览器时它应该如何表现？ 在创建任何应用程序元素时，每个设计师都应该牢记这一重要考虑因素。 三角形应该保持固定高度还是相应缩小？ 它将如何影响位于其下方的元素？ 如果设计师选择合并此元素，这些是必须在设计说明中解决的关键问题。

浮动元素

我无法计算我不得不处理这个问题的次数。 想象一下这个场景：设计师看到页面上有一点空白，然后开始思考：“我怎样才能让它更有趣？”，恰好 0.01 秒后，他想到了：“让我们添加一些浮动的东西，让开发人员讨厌我！”。

我非常讨厌他们🥲那些东西是不可能让他们有反应的。 通常，我只是在桌面上实现它们，祈祷它们适用于所有桌面屏幕尺寸，并在手机和平​​板电脑上完全删除它们。

不一致的组件

这是另一种情况。 您的团队花了整整一周的时间来努力实施由您的设计师精心制作的时尚现代的表单组件。 随着下一个冲刺的开始，您热切地等待着新联系页面的设计，却看到了令人不快的景象。 设计师似乎在追求独特性，在这个特定页面上选择了完全不同的组件。 面对这种情况，您有两种可能的选择：

  1. 休息一下，在厕所里哭泣并实施第二组组件，以混淆从事该项目的其他开发人员
  2. 尝试说服设计师使用已经实现的元素，但失败了，最后选择了第 1 条路线。 不可翻译的设计

    这是前端开发人员经常遇到的另一个熟悉的场景。 想象一下，您正在处理一个多语言页面，而英文版的设计似乎一切都很顺利。 然而，一旦您深入研究不同的语言，麻烦就开始浮出水面。 看一眼上面的设计：位于文本框后面的视觉上吸引人的偏移图像。 然而，当您切换到一种文本更简洁的语言（例如中文）或文本更宽的语言（例如德语）时，事情开始显得有些不对劲。

    那么，我们如何才能有效地解决这个问题呢？ 那么，这里的关键是与设计师的开放式沟通和协作。 与他们协商并讨论可能的解决方案至关重要。 这就是转折：我们可能只需要进行那些令人愉快的特定于语言的调整。 哦，为我们的代码增加复杂性和混乱性的乐趣！ 无论如何，当我们可以有一个错综复杂的异常网络时，谁还需要精简和干净的代码？ 然而，通过与设计师积极合作，我们可以在适应不同语言和保持代码整洁之间找到适当的平衡。 无论使用何种语言，我们都可以共同应对这些挑战并确保设计看起来很棒。

    结语

    与设计师合作的基本方面之一是创造协作环境并确保思想和经验的无缝交流。 作为参与项目的程序员，积极参与设计过程至关重要。 通过提供深思熟虑的注释并解释其背后的原因，您可以避免大量修改或复杂的设计实施，从而节省宝贵的时间。 重要的是要承认，作为开发人员，您可能并不总是对设计决策拥有最终决定权。 毕竟，应用程序是为用户而不是开发人员构建的。 牢记这一观点将有助于培养以用户为中心的方法并确保项目的最终成功。 请记住，有效的协作和接纳用户的观点是创建卓越应用程序的关键要素！

    本文中使用的示例设计可在此处获得： Figma Design 。 将它发送给您所有的设计师朋友，要么教他们一些东西，要么只是开个玩笑！ 这些只是一些烦人的编码元素。 你有兴趣了解更多吗？ 在评论中让我知道，因为相信我，我还有很多……



原文:

Obligatory (my editor made me add it) Disclaimer 😉
All scenarios presented in this article are intended purely for humorous purposes and reflect the personal opinions of the author. The intention is not to offend anyone or specifically target real situations or projects. Any resemblance to actual events or projects is purely coincidental.

Intro

Are you familiar with that feeling when you come across a design that immediately triggers a cascade of expletives in your mind, making you question every decision that brought you to that particular moment? No? Well, consider yourself fortunate. Certain design practices have the uncanny ability to evoke such reactions from front-end developers. Regrettably, encountering one of these design challenges in a project often implies a high likelihood of encountering more. In this article, we will delve into the realm of designs that pose significant obstacles during development. Brace yourself for an insightful journey into the world of frustrating design choices. Let's embark on this adventure together!

Diagonal lines

Let's dive into a common predicament that many web developers face, regardless of their experience level. CSS, known for its love affair with rectangles, tends to make life difficult when it comes to anything else. You can squeeze out some rounded corners if you're lucky, but anything beyond that requires delving into the realm of hacky solutions. And that's exactly what we're about to explore. Picture this: a well-intentioned designer decides to spice things up with a "dynamic" and "interesting" divider between different sections of a website.

There are numerous approaches to tackle this problem, but my personal preference would be to employ an SVG triangle, as demonstrated in the illustration below. Implementing this solution isn't overly complicated, but how should it behave when scaling the browser? This crucial consideration should be in every designer's mind when creating any application element. Should the triangle maintain a fixed height or scale down accordingly? How will it affect the elements positioned below it? These are the pivotal questions that must be addressed in the design notes if the designer chooses to incorporate this element.

Floating elements

I can’t count how many times I had to deal with this one. Picture this scenario: the designer sees a bit of empty space on the page and starts thinking: “How can I make it more interesting?”, after exactly 0.01 seconds he figures it out: “Let’s add some floaty stuff and make the devs hate me!”.

I hate them so much 🥲 Those things are impossible to make them responsive. Usually, I just implement them on desktops, pray that they work on all the desktop screen sizes and completely remove them on mobile and tablets.

Inconsistent components

Here is another scenario. Your team has dedicated an entire week to diligently implementing the sleek and modern form components meticulously crafted by your designer. As the next sprint kicks off, you eagerly await the designs for a new contact page, only to be greeted by a jarring sight. The designer, seemingly in a quest for uniqueness, has opted for completely different components on this particular page. Faced with this situation, you have two possible routes to take:

  1. Take a break to cry in the toilet and implement a second set of components to confuse the rest of the devs working on the project
  2. Try to persuade the designer to use the already implemented elements, fail at it and finally go with route no. 1

Untranslatable design

Here's another familiar scenario that frontend developers often encounter. Imagine you're working on a multilingual page, and everything seems to be going smoothly with the design in the English version. However, as soon as you delve into different languages, the trouble begins to rear its head. Take a glance at the design above: a visually appealing offset image positioned behind a text box. Yet, when you switch to a language with more condensed text, such as Chinese, or one with wider text, like German, things start to appear a bit off.

So, how can we address this issue effectively? Well, the key here is open communication and collaboration with the designer. It's essential to consult with them and discuss potential solutions. And here's the twist: we might just need to make those delightful language-specific adjustments. Oh, the joy of adding complexity and messiness to our code! Who needs streamlined and clean code anyway, when we can have a tangled web of exceptions? However, by actively collaborating with the designer, we can find the right balance between accommodating different languages and maintaining code cleanliness. Together, we can navigate these challenges and ensure that the design looks fantastic, regardless of the language used.

Ending notes

One of the fundamental aspects of working with designers is creating a collaborative environment and ensuring a seamless exchange of ideas and experiences. As a programmer involved in a project, it's crucial to actively participate in the design process. By providing thoughtful notes and explaining your reasoning behind them, you can save valuable time by avoiding the need for extensive revisions or complicated design implementations. It's important to acknowledge that as a developer, you may not always have the final say in design decisions. After all, applications are built for users, not for developers. Keeping this perspective in mind will help foster a user-centric approach and ensure the ultimate success of the project. Remember, effective collaboration and embracing the user's perspective are key ingredients for creating remarkable applications!

The example design used in this article is available here: Figma Design. Send it to all your designer friends to either teach them something or just have a laugh! Those are only some of the annoying elements to code. Are you interested in learning more? Let me know in the comments because believe me, I have a lot more of them…

Items.Title: 增强您的终端体验：Warp 还是 Fig？ | Enhancing your terminal experience: Warp or Fig?
Items.Description:
在使用Warp几个月之后（并收集了已经在使用它的同事的反馈），是时候了。 它比无花果好吗？

您会在这里找到一篇很长的文章，我在其中尝试比较这两种工具。 如果您不知道 Warp 或 Fig 是什么，这将是一个很好的介绍。 如果您已经知道这些工具，但无法决定使用哪一个，希望本文能帮助您做出决定。

有些功能是说得比说得好，这就是为什么您可以在这里找到一个视频，我在其中介绍了本文中涵盖的所有主题。 我建议您给它一块手表，但如果您更喜欢阅读，请继续滚动。

您将在本文中找到以下内容：

看起来很有希望，对吧？ 让我们开始吧！

主要差异

首先要明确一点，它们不是一回事。 让我强调一下主要区别：

您可以像安装常规应用程序一样安装它（因为它就是这样），单击该图标，它就会显示在您的屏幕上。
它不是独立存在的，而是作为另一种工具的扩展。

每种方法的优缺点是什么？ 举几个例子：

  • 在 Fig 上入门可能更容易，因为该工具增强了您已经熟悉的终端，而 Warp 是一个全新的终端。 但是，适应它应该不会花很长时间。 实际上，它有很多方便的定制。
  • 作为一个合适的应用程序，Warp 可以更好地控制 UI 以及用户如何与之交互，例如，您可以本地编写多行脚本并使用鼠标移动光标，这在 Fig 上是不可能的，因为它依赖于它运行的终端。
  • 您需要在计算机上安装一个应用程序，而 Warp 的设置已经在 Warp 窗口本身中进行了处理。

价钱

但是，当涉及到更多面向团队的功能时，其中一些功能需要付费计划。

它们都具有三个定价级别（免费/个人、团队和企业），主要区别在于：

经线（定价页面）

  • 免费版包括人工智能
  • 没有针对团队和企业的免费试用，但作为个人您可能甚至不需要它

图（定价页面）

  • 团队每个用户每月 12 美元，企业需要联系他们
  • 团队有 14 天的免费试用期

人工智能

让我们解决房间里的大象问题，在定价之后，2023 年最热门的话题只能是 AI。 现在让我们看看 Warp 和 Fig 在这方面的比较，这样我们就可以在剩下的帖子中忘记 AI，我保证！

如上所述，Warp 在免费版本中包含 AI，而 Fig 需要付费计划。

激活 AI 在这两个工具上的工作方式完全相同，只需以#开头的自然语言句子，它就会被发送到 AI 引擎以转换为命令。

这是它在 Warp 上的样子：

利用 Warp 是独立应用程序这一事实，UI 的处理方式更加灵活。 您可以在此处使用 Cmd+Enter 输入命令，但您也可以直接在 UI 中单击按钮以获取解释、查看示例或相关命令。

在这里，您可以与 AI 进行更强大的交互，例如，您可以要求对命令进行解释，甚至可以在您启动的命令出错时获得更多见解。

值得注意的是，尽管命令生成是免费的且没有限制，但功能更强大的 AI 侧面板在免费计划中仅限于每天 100 个请求。

这是它在图上的样子：

生成的命令完全相同，但 UI 更像终端（与 Copilot CLI 非常相似），您可以使用箭头选择下一个操作。

这些选项可以让您很好地控制答案，因为如果您不满意，您可以编辑或重新生成，但是没有快速的方法来获取有关该命令的更多信息，正如我最近在有关 AI 的文章中通常写的那样，运行一个在不理解其作用的情况下使用 AI 生成的脚本通常不是一个好主意。 如果您只需要语法，那么您就可以开始了。

特征

这两个工具具有广泛的智能功能，其中一些与两者几乎重叠，而另一些则各不相同。 让我们通过它们。

两者都可用

自动完成

就个人而言，这是我认为任何终端应用程序/工具/任何东西都必须具备的关键功能。 我无法想象此时回到没有自动完成的终端。

我们今天看到的两种工具都具有此功能（否则我不会写关于它们的文章），而且它们都工作得很好。 我真正喜欢的一点是，它们不仅限于“简单”的建议，例如在使用cd浏览文件系统时的下一个文件夹，而且它们还提供了git别名的建议，仅举一个例子。

这两种工具都能够读取我的别名，提出建议，并在工具提示中显示别名的用途。 我注意到的一点不同是 Warp 不仅在下拉菜单中显示建议，而且还会尝试猜测您的下一个命令可能是什么并将其显示在输入字段中，因此您可以决定是否浏览建议的延续下拉列表或已经接受猜测。

这里我举个例子，你可以选择一个git命令（里面有一个字母c ，因为我已经写过了）然后自己继续，或者直接点击右箭头接受git cb develop 。

说到交互，这两种工具都允许您对其进行自定义，无论您是想使用箭头、制表符、输入还是您最喜欢的任何方式来导航和选择建议。

人工智能

我答应过我不会再谈论 AI，但由于这是两种工具都可用的功能列表，让我再提一下。

好了，本章结束：）

主题

如果您不喜欢默认主题，这两种工具都支持自定义主题！

在 Warp 上，您可以创建自己的或从社区中选择一个。 您可以使用^ + Cmd + T快速选择不同的主题，或者如果您不记得快捷方式，您可以随时使用命令面板并键入“主题”。 侧边栏将在左侧打开，您可以选择要使用的主题。

只有无花果

自定义脚本

通过 Fig 设置应用程序，您可以定义一些自定义脚本。 您可以将它们视为带有一些额外功能的别名，例如添加描述和能够共享它们。

还有一个脚本商店，可以在其中分发和安装公共脚本。

要运行其中一个脚本，您可以从fig run开始，自动完成下拉列表将向您显示可用的脚本。

完整的 IDE 集成

作为插件而不是独立应用程序的优势之一是您可以更轻松地与其他工具集成。 点文件

别名、变量、路径……您必须在某些隐藏文件中手动设置的所有恼人设置都可以通过 Fig 设置应用程序以可视化方式进行管理。

使用免费计划，您可以管理您的个人点文件，而使用付费计划，您还可以管理配置并与您的团队共享。

仅变形

当涉及到 UI 和 UX 时，作为独立应用程序的优势很明显，Warp 可以更好地控制用户与其交互的方式，并且可以利用它来提供更好的体验。

命令面板

命令面板是一种在许多范围内可用的常用工具，从 MacOS 到 GitHub、Visual Studio 代码……以及 Warp。

使用Cmd + P您可以打开命令面板并搜索要运行的任何命令。 如您所料，您可以开始键入以缩小结果范围，并且可以使用箭头浏览它们。

积木

这是我在尝试之前不知道自己需要的功能。 块是一种将命令和输出分组到单个实体中的方法，可以轻松滚动具有明确定义的输入和输出的长日志。

在这个会话中，我运行了三个命令：首先是pnpm install ，它有一个很长的日志，然后是一个git status和我的一个别名来打印提交。 屏幕上有三个块，即使第一个块有很长的日志，我仍然可以在顶部看到我执行了哪个命令，以防我想调试它。 就在下方，一个带有git status输出的新块完全适合终端，最后是一个带有我的 git 别名的长日志。 我向上滚动了一点，这样我就可以一次看到所有这些，每个块的顶部都有命令。

块并与永久链接共享（即使在免费计划中），这样您就不必滚动长输出来向某人展示特定部分，您只需将整个块的链接发送给他们，他们就可以在他们的浏览器。

这是共享块的示例，您可以在其中看到顶部的命令和正确突出显示的输出： app.warp.dev/block/o2Z0EGHZoDT834ohnO5K ...

您还可以专门针对块执行一些操作，例如查找特定文本、复制输入或输出以及根据块的内容向 AI 寻求帮助。

多标签

公平地说，拥有多个选项卡是终端的责任，显然，无花果不能那样做。 如果你想要更多的标签，你需要在已经支持标签的终端上使用 fig ......举一个例子：Warp。

用户友好的输入

真正“磨练我的齿轮”的一件事是在输入文本中移动我的光标并且必须选择/剪切/粘贴其中的一部分。

我想我不是唯一的人，正如 Warp 所想的那样。 您键入命令的输入框是一个正确的输入字段，您可以使用鼠标单击输入中您想要的任何位置，所有预期的文本编辑器键盘快捷键也应该有效（例如，Option + left to move到前一个词）。

加分项，可以使用多光标！

技术

撇开玩笑不谈，很高兴看到 Rust 如何变得越来越流行，并且它被用来提供又酷又快的工具。

开源

这两个项目都关注开源社区，即使核心代码不可用，每个项目在 GitHub 上都有近十几个公共存储库。

经

组织页面 - warpdotdev

在拥有大约 13k 星的主仓库之后，您可以找到主题仓库。 这是 Warp 官方主题所在的地方，您确实可以参与其中并做出贡献。 这里使用的编程语言是Python。

感觉受到启发？ 您可以使用社区成员创建的这个工具 100% 自定义您自己的主题： Warp Themes 。 您可以使用所有颜色选择器生成它，查看实时预览，然后下载可以导入 Warp 的文件。

工作流回购也很有趣，这次使用 Rust，它包含所有公共工作流的集合，您可以从 Warp（命令面板，或^ + Shift + R ）以及从commands.dev访问。

如图

图组织页面-withfig

这是所有自动完成内容的来源，如果命令丢失或过时，我们非常欢迎您打开合并请求。

与 Warp 的工作流存储库类似，在插件存储库中，您可以找到 Fig 的插件存储库的内容。 你有一个很酷的插件可以与社区分享吗？ 开个公关！

文档

这两种工具都带有很好的文档，其中有大量的屏幕截图、示例和 gif。 我注意到在 Warp 上也有一些视频，感觉不错。

安装说明清晰明了，所有核心功能都得到了适当的记录。

更新日志也可用，这始终是一个健康项目的好兆头：

  • 经线变更日志
  • 图更新日志

社区

项目成功的关键之一是它周围的社区。 让我们来看看一些数字：

公制经如图
推特关注者不和谐成员通过查看统计数据，Fig 在 Twitter 上的关注者略多，而所有其他社交媒体都认为 Warp 处于领先地位。 最大的不同是在 Discord 社区中 warp 是 10 倍，在 YouTube 上 warp 分享了一些很酷的教育内容（比如5 Command Line Tools That Boost Developer Productivity (2023)和Terminal Basics That Nobody Teaches You In School ，很酷的标题 :D ) 而 fig 似乎根本没有使用 YouTube。 不过他们在 TikTok 上都很活跃！

这里没有指标，但这两种工具都有自己的博客 ( Warp - Fig )，他们在博客中谈论他们的产品、构建产品时面临的挑战以及其他有趣的话题。

结论

现在我们已经进入了最后一章，让我们回到最初的陈述：我们正在研究两种不同的工具。

由于块、命令面板和适当的输入字段等功能，Warp 带来了与终端交互的全新体验。 但是，这伴随着移动到新终端的要求。 这是个问题吗？ 可能不会，这取决于你的习惯。

另一方面，Fig 旨在提供一组新功能，即使不如 Warp，但已经在您最喜欢的终端中。

从我的角度来看，这可能是在两者之间进行选择时要考虑的最大标准之一：您是否需要继续使用您的终端并进行一些改进，或者您是否愿意切换到新终端以访问所有额外功能？

很难说对与错，因为这两种工具都很棒……答案 100% 取决于您。

话虽如此，感谢您阅读到这里！ 我希望本文能让您对这两种工具有一个很好的概述，并帮助您做出决定。

经线： www.warp.dev/
图： fig.io/

感谢您阅读本文，希望您觉得它有趣！

你喜欢我的内容吗？ 您可以考虑订阅我的 YouTube 频道！ 这对我来说意义重大❤️ 你可以在这里找到它：



原文:

After using Warp for over a couple of months (and collecting feedback from colleagues already using it), the time has come. Is it better than Fig?

What you will find here is a quite long article where I try to compare the two tools. If you don't know what Warp or Fig are, this will be a nice introduction. If you already know the tools and can't decide which one to use, I hope this will help you make a decision.

Some of the features are better shown than told, that's why you can find here a video where I go through all the topics I cover in this article. I'd recommend you give it a watch, but if you prefer reading, keep scrolling.

What you're going to find in this article:

Seems promising, right? Let's get started!

Key differences

First of all, let's make it clear, they're not the same thing. Let me highlight the main differences:

Warp is a terminal: to make it simple, an app that lives on its own. You can install it as you would do with a regular app (because that's what it is), click on the icon and it will show up on your screen.
Fig is a plugin that lives on top of supported terminals, IDEs, and shells. It does not live on its own but rather as an extension of another tool.

What are the pros and cons of each approach? To name a few:

  • Onboarding on Fig might be easier as the tool enhances a terminal you're already familiar with, while Warp is an entire new terminal. However, it shouldn't take long to get used to it. Actually, it has quite some handy customizations.
  • Being a proper app, Warp has more control on the UI and how the user interacts with it, for example, you can natively write multiline scripts and use the mouse to move the cursor around, something that wouldn't be possible on Fig as it relies on the terminal where is it running.
  • Fig's flexibility from working on top of a terminal might make it easier to distribute on multiple platforms, however, both Fig and Warp are still only available on MacOS. Fig has a waitlist for Windows and Linux and the same for Warp, but the latter also mentions the plan to support WASM with the goal of running on the browser to enable collaborative features.
  • Fig's settings clearly aren't managed through the terminal, even if we said it's a plugin. You need an app installed on your computer, while Warp's settings are already handled in the Warp window itself.

Pricing

Both Warp and Fig are free to use, and already ship a lot of interesting features for individuals. However, when it comes to more team-oriented features, some of them require a paid plan.

They both have three levels of pricing (Free/Individual, Team and Enterprise), with the main differences being:

Warp (pricing page)

  • Free version includes AI
  • Pricing for Team and Enterprise is not available yet, you can get in touch with them to get a quote
  • No free trial for Team and Enterprise, but as an individual you probably won't even need it

Fig (pricing page)

  • AI is only for paid plans
  • Team is $12 per user per month, Enterprise requires contacting them
  • Team has a 14 day free trial

Artificial Intelligence

Let's address the elephant in the room, right after pricing the hottest topic in 2023 can only be AI. Let's see how Warp and Fig compare in this area now, so that we can forget about AI for the rest of the post, I promise!

As mentioned above, Warp includes AI in the free version, while Fig requires a paid plan.

Activating the AI works exactly the same on both tools, just start a sentence in natural language with # and it will be sent to the AI engine to be converted into a command.

Here is how it would look like on Warp:

Leveraging the fact that Warp is a standalone app, there's more flexibility in how the UI is handled. Here you can input the command with Cmd+Enter but you can also click on buttons to get an explanation, see examples or related commands, directly in the UI.

Asking for an explanation will the Warp AI side panel. Here you can get a more powerful interaction with the AI, for example you can ask for an explanation of the command or even get more insights when a command you launched resulted in an error.

It's worth noting that despite the command generation being free and having no limits, the more powerful AI side panel is limited to 100 requests per day on the free plan.

And here is how it would look like in Fig:

The generated command is exactly the same, but the UI is more terminal-like (really similar to Copilot CLI) and you can select the next action with the arrows.

The options give you good control over the answer as you can either edit or regenerate if you're not satisfied, but there's no quick way to get more information about the command and as I usually write in my articles about AI these days, running an AI-generated script without understanding what it does is usually not a good idea. If all you needed was just the syntax, then you're good to go.

Features

The two tools come with a wide range of smart features, some of them are pretty much overlapping to both, and some are unique to each one. Let's go through them.

Available on both

Autocomplete

Personally, this is the key feature I consider a must-have for any terminal app/tool/whatever. I can't imagine going back to a terminal without autocomplete at this point.

Both tools we're looking at today have this feature (otherwise I wouldn't be writing about them), and they both work pretty well. Something I really like is that they aren't limited to "simple" suggestions such as the next folder while navigating the file system with cd, but they also provide suggestions for git aliases, to name one.

Both tools are able to read my aliases, suggest them and also show in a tooltip what command the alias is for. Warp suggests alias expansions as well, they posted a demo in a tweet recently.

A little difference I noticed is that Warp does not only show the suggestions in a dropdown menu, but also tries to guess what your next command might be and shows it already in the input field, so you can decide if navigate through the suggested continuations in the dropdown or already accept the guess.

Let me add an example here, you can either select a git command (with a letter c in it, as that's what I already written) and continue by yourself, or directly click the right arrow to accept git cb develop in one go.

Speaking of the interaction, both tools allow you to customize it, whether you want to use the arrows, tab, enter or whatever you like the most to navigate and select the suggestions.

AI

I promised I wouldn't talk about AI anymore, but since this is a list of features available on both tools, let me mention it again.

Ok, end of this chapter :)

Themes

If you don't like the default theme, both tools support custom ones!

On Warp you can create your own or choose one from the community. There's a public repo on GitHub about that!

You can quickly select a different theme with ^ + Cmd + T or if you don't remember the shortcut, you can always use the command palette and type "theme". A sidebar will open on the left and you can select the theme you want to use.

Pretty much the same happens on Fig, you can find the themes on the settings and there's also a public repo on GitHub.

Fig Only

Custom scripts

Through the Fig settings app you can define some custom scripts. You can see them as aliases with some extra features around them, such as adding a description and being able to share them.

There's also a Scripts Store where public scripts can be distributed and installed.

To run one of those scripts you can start with fig run and the autocomplete dropdown will show you the available ones.

Full IDEs integration

One of the advantages of being a plugin instead of a standalone app is that you can more easily integrate with other tools. Fig currently works with VS Code, Android Studio and the JetBrains IDEs.

Dotfiles

Aliases, variables, paths... all the annoying settings you have to manually set up in some hidden files can be managed through the Fig settings app in a visual way.

With the free plan, you can manage your personal dotfiles, while with the paid plans you can also manage and share the configs with your team.

Warp Only

The advantages of being a standalone app are clear when it comes to the UI and UX, Warp has more control over how the user interacts with it and it can leverage that to provide a much better experience.

Command Palette

Command Palettes are a common tool available in many scopes, from MacOS, to GitHub, Visual Studio code... and also Warp.

With Cmd + P you can open the command palette and search for any command you want to run. As you might expect, you can start typing to narrow down the results and you can use the arrows to navigate through them.

Blocks

This is the feature I didn't know I needed until I tried it. Blocks are a way to group commands and outputs into a single entity, making it easy to scroll on long logs with clearly defined input and output.

On this session I ran three commands: pnpm install first, which has a long log, then a git status and one of my aliases to print commits. There are three blocks on the screen and even if the first has a reeally long log, I can still see on top which command I executed in case I want to debug it. Right below, a new block with the git status output which fits entirely into the terminal and last, one more long log with my git alias. I scrolled up a little bit so that I can see all of them at once, with the commands on top of each block.

Blocks and also be shared with permalinks (even in the free plan) so that instead of having to scroll through a long output to show someone a specific part, you can just send them the link to the entire block and they can see it in their browser.

Here's an example of a shared block, where you can see the command on top and the output properly highlighted: app.warp.dev/block/o2Z0EGHZoDT834ohnO5K…

You can also run some actions specifically on blocks, such as finding a specific text, copying the input or output and asking AI for assistance based on the block's content.

Multi tabs

To be fair, having multiple tabs is the responsibility of the terminal, clearly, fig cannot do that. If you want more tabs, you need to use fig on top of a terminal that already supports tabs... to name one: Warp.

User-friendly Input

One thing that really "grinds my gears" is moving my cursor in the input text and having to select/cut/paste parts of it.

I think I'm not the only one though, as Warp thought about that. The input box where you type your command is a proper input field, you can use the mouse to click anywhere you want in your input, and all your expected text-editor keyboard shortcuts should work as well (for example, Option + left to move to the previous word).

Bonus points, you can use multi-cursors!

Technologies

Warp has a lot of stuff to handle so the team decided to build it in Rust, which rhymes to "blazingly fast". Jokes aside, it's cool to see how Rust is becoming more popular and it's being used to deliver cool and fast tools.

Fig is built in Typescript making it easy to have the dashboard also available as a web app in your browser.

Open Source

Both projects have an eye on the open source community, even if the core code isn't available, with almost a dozen of public repos on GitHub each.

Warp

Organization page - warpdotdev

Right after the main repo with about 13k stars, you can find the themes repository. This is where Warp official themes are and indeed you can jump in and contribute. The programming language used here is Python.

Feeling inspired? You can 100% customize your own theme with this tool created by a community member: Warp Themes. You can generate it with all the color pickers, see the live preview and then download a file you can then import in Warp.

The workflows repo is also interesting, using Rust this time, holding a collection of all public workflows you can access from Warp (Command palette, or ^ + Shift + R) and also from commands.dev.

Fig

Fig organization page - withfig

Fig's coolest repo is for sure the autocomplete with almost 22k stars. This is where all the autocomplete content comes from and if a command is missing or outdated, you're more than welcome in opening a Pull Request.

Similarly to Warp's workflows repo, on the plugins repository, you can find the content of Fig's plugin store. Do you have a cool plugin to share with the community? Open a PR!

Documentation

Warp docs - Fig docs

Both tools come with good documentation, with a lot of screenshots, examples, and gifs. I noticed on Warp there are also some videos, which is a nice touch.

Installation instructions are clear and all core features are properly documented.

Changelogs are also available, which is always a good sign of a healthy project:

  • Warp changelog
  • Fig changelog

Community

One of the keys to success for a project is the community around it. Let's take a look at some numbers:

Metric Warp Fig
GitHub followers 918 477
Twitter followers 14k 16k
Discord members 10k 1k
dev.to user posts ~60 ~50
YouTube subscribers 2k 24
TikTok followers 6.5k 4.3k

By looking at the stats, Fig has slightly more followers on twitter while all other socials see Warp in the lead. The biggest difference is in the Discord community with warp being 10x more and on YouTube where warp shares some cool educational content (like 5 Command Line Tools That Boost Developer Productivity (2023) and Terminal Basics That Nobody Teaches You In School, cool titles :D) while fig doesn't seem to be using YouTube at all. They're both quite active on TikTok though!

No metrics here, but both tools have their own blog (Warp - Fig) where they talk about their product, challenges faced in building it, and other interesting topics.

Warp also has some swags like t-shirts, hoodies and even a jacket (tweet with the details).

Conclusion

Now that we're in the closing chapter, let's get back to the initial statement: we're looking at two different tools.

Warp is bringing a whole new experience in interacting with a terminal thanks to features like blocks, command palette and the proper input field. However, this comes with the requirement of moving to a new terminal. Is this a problem? Probably not, it depends on your habits.

On the other side, Fig aims to provide a set of new features, even if less than Warp, but already in your favourite terminal.

From my point of view this is probably one the biggest criteria to consider when choosing between the two: do you need to keep using your terminal with some improvements or are you willing to switch to a new one in order to access all the extra features?

It's hard to say what is right or wrong as both tools are great... the answer is 100% up to you.

And with that said, thank you for reading until this point! I hope this article gave you a good overview of the two tools and helped you in making a decision.

Warp: www.warp.dev/
Fig: fig.io/

Thanks for reading this article, I hope you found it interesting!

Items.Title: 管理复杂性：工程师的角色 | Managing Complexity: The Role of the Engineer
Items.Description:
软件开发动态认识到软件的演进通常受到两个关键因素的限制：组织稳定性和对系统内工作的开发人员的理解。

  1. 这意味着软件系统的增长不能超过其开发人员理解其复杂性的能力或组织适应这种演变的能力。 当任何一个因素滞后时，它都会产生约束，从而阻止软件进化的进程。

  2. 因此，软件演进不再是纯粹的技术进步，而是更多地关注推动这些进步的人和他们运行的环境。

  3. 当谈到软件系统的复杂性时，一个固有的事实是它们往往会随着时间的推移而增加并成为障碍，阻碍软件的顺利运行。

  4. 但是，必须认识到，管理这种复杂性并不需要对整个组织有透彻的了解。 相反，它是关于对系统的信任，相信它会按预期运行。 与其试图将组织作为一个整体来理解，不如将重点放在迭代改进系统上。 它是关于在不破坏其任何核心特征的情况下进行增量调整以提升系统。 这种方法降低了复杂性，同时确保系统保持有效。

这使我们认识到工程师在系统中的关键作用。 作为一名工程师，您的任务是确定系统的关键特征并了解影响它们的因素。

这实质上是映射项目，创建对软件系统功能的全面理解。 借助这张地图，工程师可以做出经过深思熟虑的决策，以驾驭日益复杂的系统，确定需要改进的领域，同时确保保留核心功能。

最好的工程师是那些拥有最准确的项目地图的人。

原文:

Software development dynamic recognizes that the evolution of software is often confined by two key factors: organizational stability and the understanding of the developers working within a system.

  1. What this means is that the growth of a software system cannot outpace the ability of its developers to comprehend its intricacies or the organization's capacity to accommodate this evolution. When either factor lags, it creates a constraint, halting the progress of software evolution.

  2. Hence, software evolution becomes less about pure technological advancements and more about the people fueling those advancements and the environment in which they operate.

  3. When it comes to the complexity of software systems, an inherent truth is that they tend to increase over time and becomes an obstacle, hampering the smooth functioning of the software.

  4. However, one must recognize that managing this complexity does not necessitate a thorough understanding of the entire organization. Instead, it's about having trust in the system, believing that it's functioning as intended. Rather than attempting to comprehend the organization as a whole, focus should be placed on improving the system iteratively. It's about making incremental adjustments that elevate the system without undermining any of its core characteristics. This approach reduces complexity while ensuring the system remains effective.

This leads us to the critical role of an engineer in the system. As an engineer, your task is to identify the key characteristics of the system and understand the factors influencing them.

This is essentially mapping the project, creating a comprehensive understanding of how the software system functions. With this map, an engineer can make calculated decisions to navigate the increasing complexity of the system, identifying areas for improvement while ensuring the core functionalities are preserved.

The best engineers are the ones who have the most accurate map of the project.

They are the ones who can navigate the complexities of the evolving system while maintaining its critical aspects. They are the ones who can effectively guide the evolution of software within the constraints of organizational stability and the collective understanding of the developers. These engineers are the true drivers of effective software evolution, skillfully balancing growth and complexity in a constant dance of innovation.

Items.Title: 微服务：如何使用空对象模式提升用户体验 | Microservices: How to use Null Object Pattern to improve user experience
Items.Description:
• 什么是空对象模式？
  • 如何申请
  • 如何实施
  • 测试非关键依赖项
  • 结论

微服务架构的弹性来自于它能够快速从故障中恢复，并能够在恢复过程中服务客户。 所有这一切都应该在不会显着降低客户体验的情况下发生。 空对象模式有助于针对后者——确保当系统的非必要部分不工作时，客户体验不会降低太多。 这种模式可以应用于技术堆栈的许多级别：从前端组件的服务不可用时开始（参见带状态栏的图片示例），当您无法获取其中的对象或类的数据时结束后端的（微）服务。


图 1. 状态栏块失败导致整个网页拒绝服务的示例

如何申请

  1. 您需要列出从业务角度来看至关重要的网页、应用程序屏幕、API 端点等。
  2. 为每个可能导致拒绝服务的项目填写所有外部依赖项的列表。
  3. 确定这种依赖性对于用户的意图是否真的很关键（例如，如果加载推荐项目失败应该阻止结帐过程）。
  4. 首先导致更多故障的非关键服务。

如何实施

实现这个模式很简单。 如果您的方法（例如工厂或存储库）无法检索对象的数据，只需返回一个空对象。 对于您的语言的实现，请查看维基百科，您可以在下面查看图表以了解想法：


图 2. 实现 Null Object 模式的接口图

然而，盲目地在任何地方应用这种模式是一个坏主意，特别是如果稍后在修改查询中使用对象的数据......假设您有一个存储库，可以从其他服务中为用户检索数据。 如果用户检索失败，那么您的存储库将返回一个具有默认值的空对象（ID 设置为 0，Name 设置为“空名称”等）。 您不能使用它来编写查询，否则您的业务逻辑将不一致。 那么如何处理呢？

您的选择之一是将用户对象的接口分为两种类型：一种可以由 Null Object 实现，另一种则不能。 这种分离对于确保永远不会在编写查询中使用空对象至关重要。

测试非关键依赖项

对于具有明确限界上下文的服务（例如推荐块），它相对容易实现，但是对于像用户配置文件这样的商品服务，它并不是那么简单，需要大量的努力才能将其转变为非关键依赖。 发生这种情况是因为对后端的每个请求通常都以查询这些服务以呈现名称或其他（在大多数情况下通常是）琐碎信息而告终。 为确保所有这些努力没有白费，明智的做法是通过引入非关键依赖性测试来强制执行此类行为。

实现此类测试的一种方法是使它们成为端到端回归测试套件的特例。 唯一的区别是我们将我们想要人为失败的依赖项的流量重新路由到模拟失败行为的服务，例如响应错误或 5 秒后超时。 请参见下图中的示例：

图 3. 测试模拟用户服务的示例

行为。 模拟测试发出带有标头的请求以关闭用户服务。 这个标头向下传播到堆栈，当到达用户服务时，它被重新路由到具有模拟行为的模拟

对于此实现，我们需要一个根据请求标头重新路由流量的 sidecar 和实现不良行为的模拟服务。

结论

空对象模式对于范围良好的依赖项绝对是必须的，同时对于商品依赖项来说可能是一个负担，因为它需要大量的工程时间来实现。

请记住，这种模式只是对最终用户“隐藏”故障，而断路器和健康检查等其他模式实际上是在尝试恢复。 因此，在必要时应用它，不要忘记在空对象模式触发时为每种情况设置它。

原文:

   
  • What is a Null Object Pattern?
  • How to apply
  • How to implement
  • Testing noncritical dependencies
  • Conclusion

Resilience of microservice architecture comes from its ability to quickly recover from failures and being able to service customers in the process of recovery. And all this should happen without significant degradation of customer experience. The Null Object Pattern helps to target the latter - ensure that customer experience doesn’t degrade much when a non-essential part of the system is not working. This pattern could be applied in many levels of the tech stack: starting from when a service for a frontend component is not available (see pic example with status bar) and finishing up when you can't get data for an object or a class within a (micro) service in the backend.


Pic 1. Example when failure of status-bar block causes service denial for whole web page

How to apply

  1. you need to come up with a list of web pages, app screens, API endpoints, etc which are crucial from a business perspective.
  2. fill a list of all external dependencies for each item that can cause a denial of service.
  3. decide if this dependency is actually critical for the user's intent (e.g. if failure of loading recommended items should block the checkout process).
  4. noncritical services that cause more failures first.

How to implement

Implementing this pattern is straightforward. If your method (such as a factory or repository) can not retrieve data for the object, simply return a null object. For the implementation in your language check out wikipedia and below you can see the diagram to get the idea:


Pic 2. Interface diagram of implementing Null Object pattern

However, blindly applying this pattern everywhere is a bad idea especially if the object's data is used later in modification queries... Imagine you have a repository that retrieves data for a user from the other service. If retrieval of the user fails, then your repository returns a null object with defaulted values (ID is set to 0, Name is set to “empty name” and so on). You can’t use it for writing queries otherwise your business logic will be inconsistent. So how to deal with this?

One of the options you have is to split your interfaces of the user object into two types: ones that could be implemented by Null Object and others that can’t. This separation is crucial to make sure that Null Object will never be used in writing queries.

Testing noncritical dependencies

It is relatively easy to implement for services with clear bounded contexts (e.g. recommendation block), however for commodity services like a user profile it isn’t that simple and requires a lot of hard work to turn it into a noncritical dependency. This happens because every request to the backend usually ends up with querying these services to render a name or other (in most cases usually) trivial information. To make sure that all this effort is not wasted it’s wise to enforce such behavior by introducing noncritical dependency testing.

One way to implement this type of testing is to make them a special case of your e2e regression test suite. The only difference is we re-route traffic for the dependency we want to artificially fail to a service that mocks failing behavior like responding with error or times out after 5 sec for example. See the example in the picture below:

Pic 3. Example of testing mocking user service with bad

behavior. Mock tests make a request with a header to turn off user-service. This header got propagated down the stack and when reaches user-service it’s got rerouted to mock with mocked behavior

For this implementation, we need a sidecar that reroutes traffic based on the request headers and the mock service which implements bad behavior.

Conclusion

Null Object Pattern is absolutely must have for well-scoped dependencies and at the same time could be a burden to implement for commodity ones as it would require a lot of engineering hours to implement.

Keep in mind that this pattern is just “hiding” the failures from the end user while others like circuit breaker, and health checks are actually trying to recover. So apply it when it’s necessary and don’t forget to set it up for each case when the null object pattern triggers.

Further reading

Items.Title: 实体框架中的实体类型 | Types of Entities in Entity Framework
Items.Description:
在实体框架（EF）中，常用的实体主要有以下三种：

  1. 持久实体：这些实体代表数据模型并对应于数据库中的表。 它们被映射到数据库表，并与底层数据库模式具有一对一的映射。 持久实体通常使用属性进行注释或使用流畅的 API 进行配置以定义它们到数据库表的映射。

  2. 复杂类型：复杂类型是在持久实体中用作属性的类。 它们没有映射到数据库中自己的表，而是作为父实体表的一部分存储。 复杂类型用于封装逻辑上属于一起的一组相关属性。

  3. 枚举（Enums）：枚举是 EF 中的一种特殊类型的实体，代表一组固定的命名值。 它们可用于定义具有一组有限可能值的持久实体的属性。 枚举通常用于为某些属性提供更有意义和类型安全的表示。

这三种类型的实体是实体框架中用于对数据库中的数据进行建模和交互的基本构建块。

    • 这些只是说明实体框架中不同类型实体的示例。 实际上，您会根据应用程序的要求和域模型定义自己的实体。

原文:

In Entity Framework (EF), there are mainly three types of entities that are commonly used:

  1. Persistent Entities: These entities represent the data model and correspond to tables in the database. They are mapped to database tables and have a one-to-one mapping with the underlying database schema. Persistent entities are typically annotated with attributes or configured using fluent API to define their mappings to database tables.

  2. Complex Types: Complex types are classes that are used as properties within persistent entities. They are not mapped to their own tables in the database but rather stored as part of the parent entity's table. Complex types are used to encapsulate a group of related properties that logically belong together.

  3. Enumerations (Enums): Enums are a special type of entity in EF that represent a fixed set of named values. They can be used to define properties on persistent entities that have a limited set of possible values. Enums are often used to provide more meaningful and type-safe representations for certain properties.

These three types of entities are the basic building blocks used in Entity Framework to model and interact with data in a database.

Certainly! Here are the types of entities in Entity Framework with examples:

  1. Persistent Entities:
    • Person: Represents a person with properties like Id, Name, Age, etc. 
 public class Person { public int Id { get; set; } public string Name { get; set; } public int Age { get; set; } }
  • Product: Represents a product with properties like Id, Name, Price, etc. 
 public class Product { public int Id { get; set; } public string Name { get; set; } public decimal Price { get; set; } }
  1. Complex Types:
    • Address: Represents an address with properties like Street, City, State, etc. Used as a complex type within another entity. 
 public class Address { public string Street { get; set; } public string City { get; set; } public string State { get; set; } }
  • OrderItem: Represents an item in an order with properties like ProductId, Quantity, Price, etc. Used as a complex type within the Order entity. 
 public class OrderItem { public int ProductId { get; set; } public int Quantity { get; set; } public decimal Price { get; set; } }
  1. Enumerations (Enums):
    • Gender: Represents the gender of a person with values Male, Female, and Other. 
 public enum Gender { Male, Female, Other }
  • OrderStatus: Represents the status of an order with values Pending, Shipped, Delivered, etc. 
 public enum OrderStatus { Pending, Shipped, Delivered }

These are just examples to illustrate the different types of entities in Entity Framework. In practice, you would define your own entities based on your application's requirements and domain model.

Items.Title: 如何为 Vue.js 和 Node.js 应用程序实现全栈日志记录解决方案 | How to Implement a Full Stack Logging Solution for a Vue.js and Node.js App
Items.Description:
但是，如果您想在浏览器控制台之外访问日志信息，比如在文件或数据库等持久数据存储中，该怎么办？ 持久化日志对于理解您的应用程序非常有帮助。 它使您能够轻松跟踪活动、调试错误并大致了解应用程序中发生的情况和时间——所有这些对于维护健康、运行良好的应用程序都至关重要。

作为 Major League Hacking Fellowship 的一部分，我被安排参与 Program Equity 的开源应用程序 Amplify 的工作。 对于这个项目，我在生产中实施了一个完整的堆栈日志记录解决方案，以解决后端操作完成和前端页面呈现之间的间歇性竞争条件。 虽然有些库可以同时处理后端和前端日志记录，但我选择为我的前端和后端记录器使用单独的库，特别是 Winston，一个用于后端的 Node.js 记录器，以及前端的 vue-logger-plugin。

本文将向您展示如何通过 API 端点连接客户端和服务器端记录器，并将来自 Vue.js 前端的日志消息保存在 Node.js 后端中。

创建一个完整的堆栈日志记录解决方案

第 1 步：创建 API 端点

首先，创建一个将接收日志记录请求的 API 端点。 我选择使用 Express 和express. Router() 在下面的代码示例中，此端点是api/event_logger/log 。

 // event_logger.js: const express = require('express') const router = express.Router() router.post('/log', async (req, res) => { // Destructure request body sent by client const { severity, data } = req.body // Send response back to client res.send({severity, data}) }) module.exports = router

要测试您的端点是否按预期工作，我建议使用 Postman，这是一个免费的开发人员工具，可以轻松地使用任何请求正文向任何端点发出 API 请求。 首先，我们将测试您是否可以接收和发送日志消息。 在 Postman 中创建一个具有{severity, data}作为“Body”的 JSON 对象，具有严重性的日志记录级别（即“info”）和数据的测试消息（即“Hello World”）。 当您在 Postman 中选择“发送”时，您应该会看到此 JSON 对象的响应。

第 2 步：创建后端记录器

接下来，我们将构建一个后端记录器。 对于这种情况，我们将使用 Winston，因为它是一个非阻塞日志库，可以记录从纯文本文件到 SASS 服务的任何内容。 这个记录器将有两种模式，文件和控制台。 温斯顿将这些模式称为“运输”。 文件传输将记录到您选择的文件——在本例中为error.log而控制台传输将记录到浏览器控制台。 我们不希望我们的最终用户在控制台中看到随机的日志消息，因此控制台传输只会在开发环境中启用。 您可以根据项目需要调整格式。

 backend_logger.js: require('dotenv').config() const { format, createLogger, transports } = require('winston') const { timestamp, combine, errors, json, colorize, printf } = format const logger = createLogger({ format: combine(timestamp(), errors({ stack: true }), json()), // Specify the name of the file to write to here transports: [new transports.File({ filename: 'error.log' })] }) // create log format for when we're in development const logFormat = printf(({ level, message, timestamp, stack }) => { return `${timestamp} ${level}: ${stack || message}` }) // if we're not in production, then log to the console if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') { logger.add( new transports.Console({ format: combine( // add color depending on log level colorize(), timestamp({ format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss' }), // include stack-trace errors({ stack: true }), logFormat ), defaultMeta: { service: 'user-service' } }) ) } module.exports = logger

在您的 API 端点中，需要您创建的后端记录器并开始向其记录消息。 在这种情况下，因为我们使用的是 Winston，所以我们将使用logger.info来记录我们从请求正文中提取的消息。

 event_logger.js: const express = require('express') const router = express.Router() const logger = require('../../utilities/backend_logger') router.post('/log', async (req, res) => { // Destructure request body sent by client const { severity, data } = req.body // Send response status of 200 back to client res.sendStatus(200) // send log messages to back-end logger logger.info(JSON.stringify({ severity, data })) }) module.exports = router

和以前一样，我们可以使用 Postman 来测试它是否按预期工作。 从上面发送相同的 POST 请求。 在 Postman 中，您应该看到“OK”的响应，因为您正在发回 200 的响应状态。

如果您正在开发中，您还应该看到一条消息记录到浏览器控制台。

第 3 步：创建前端记录器

我发现由于创建了太多的 polyfill，Winston 很难在 Vue.js 前端中使用。 因此，您需要一个单独的前端记录器，以及一种与后端对话的方式。

对于客户端记录器，我们将使用vue-logger-plugin 。 我喜欢这个库，因为它允许您创建自定义挂钩。 在这种情况下，我们将构建一个自定义的 after hook，它利用另一个库 axios 将日志消息发送到您的 API 端点。

它提供了一种向服务器发送异步请求的简单方法，这意味着客户端代码可以在发送请求和等待响应的同时继续运行。 这对于日志消息很重要，日志消息通常在后台发送并且不应中断主应用程序逻辑。

 frontend_logger.js: import VueLogger from 'vue-logger-plugin' import axios from 'axios' // after hook to send log messages to api endpoint const ServerLogHook = { run: (event) => { axios.post('/api/event_logger/log', { severity: event.level, data: event.argumentArray }) } } // define options const options = { enabled: true, level: 'debug', afterHooks: [ServerLogHook] } // export logger with applied options export default new VueLogger(options)

接下来，我们将通过将其附加到我们的应用程序来使我们的新前端记录器可供使用。

 main.js: import Vue from 'vue'; import App from './App.vue'; // import front-end logging import vueLogger from './utilities/frontend_logger' // for using front-end logger Vue.use(vueLogger) new Vue({ render: (h) => h(App) }).$mount('#app');

附加后， $log和$logger属性将在您的 Vue 应用程序中全局可用。 一种简单的测试方法是将this.$log或this.$logger直接输入到您的 Vue 实例中，这样您就会在加载 Vue 应用程序时看到这些测试消息。

 main.js: new Vue({ created: function() { const testObject = { name: 'test', value: 'this is a test object' } // using $log this.$log.debug('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.info('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.warn('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.error('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.log('Test Message', testObject) // using $logger this.$logger.debug('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.info('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.warn('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.error('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.log('Test Message', testObject) } })

第 4 步：将它们连接在一起

Now that all the pieces are in place, let's try this out. Fire up your application in development. You should see the test log messages appear in the console and in the `error.log` file, which we set in the Winston logger. You're now free to add logging statements anywhere you please in your application on the frontend with `this.$log` and `this.$logger` and on the backend by including and invoking the Winston logger.

结论

总之，您可以通过创建将日志消息从前端记录器传递到后端记录器的 API 端点来创建完整堆栈日志记录解决方案。 然后后端记录器将日志消息保存到一个文件中，并可选择打印到控制台。 对于 Vue.js 和 Node.js 应用程序，我在前端使用了 vue-logger-plugin，在后端使用了 Winston。 使用 Postman 一路测试您的 API 端点。

使用 Winston，您可以创建额外的自定义传输以将信息保存到您选择的存储位置，例如数据库。

试试看，如果您这样做了，请对您创建的内容发表评论！



原文:

JavaScript developers are familiar withconsole.log(),which writes output to your browser console. But what if you want to access logging information outside the browser console, say in a persisted data store like a file or database? Persisting logs can be incredibly helpful for understanding your application. It enables you to easily track activity, debug errors, and generally understand what happens and when in your application – all critical for maintaining a healthy, well-functioning app.

As part of the Major League Hacking Fellowship, I was matched with working on Program Equity’s open source app Amplify. For this project, I implemented a full stack logging solution in production to address an intermittent race condition between a back-end operation completing, and a front-end page render. While there are libraries that can handle both back- and front-end logging, I opted to use separate libraries for my front-end and back-end loggers, specifically Winston, a logger for Node.js, for the back-end, and vue-logger-plugin for the front-end.

This article will show you how to connect client and server-side loggers through an API endpoint and persist log messages from your Vue.js front-end in your Node.js back-end.

Creating a Full Stack Logging Solution

Step 1: Creating the API Endpoint

First, create an API endpoint that will receive logging requests. I opted to use Express and express. Router().Since we’ll be writing to a data store, a creative act, you’ll want to make this a POST route. In the code examples below, this endpoint is api/event_logger/log.

// event_logger.js: const express = require('express')
const router = express.Router() router.post('/log', async (req, res) => { // Destructure request body sent by client const { severity, data } = req.body // Send response back to client res.send({severity, data})
}) module.exports = router

To test that your endpoint is working as expected, I recommend using Postman, a free developer tool that makes it easy to make API requests to any endpoint with any request body. First, we’ll test whether you can receive and send a log message. Create a JSON object with {severity, data} as the “Body” in Postman with a logging level for severity (i.e. “info”) and a test message for data (i.e. “Hello World”). You should see a response of this JSON object when you select “Send” in Postman.

Step 2: Creating the Backend Logger

Next, we’ll build a back-end logger. For this case, we’ll use Winston because it is a non-blocking logging library that can log to anything from a plain text file to SASS services. This logger will have two modes, File and Console. Winston refers to these modes as “transports”. File transport will log to a file of your choice – in this case, error.log – while Console transport will log to the browser console. We don’t want our end users to see random log messages in the console, so the Console transport will only be enabled in the development environment. You can adjust the format as needed for your project.

backend_logger.js:
require('dotenv').config()
const { format, createLogger, transports } = require('winston')
const { timestamp, combine, errors, json, colorize, printf } = format const logger = createLogger({ format: combine(timestamp(), errors({ stack: true }), json()), // Specify the name of the file to write to here transports: [new transports.File({ filename: 'error.log' })]
}) // create log format for when we're in development
const logFormat = printf(({ level, message, timestamp, stack }) => { return `${timestamp} ${level}: ${stack || message}`
}) // if we're not in production, then log to the console
if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') { logger.add( new transports.Console({ format: combine( // add color depending on log level colorize(), timestamp({ format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss' }), // include stack-trace errors({ stack: true }), logFormat ), defaultMeta: { service: 'user-service' } }) )
} module.exports = logger

In your API endpoint, require the back-end logger that you created and start logging messages to it. In this case, since we’re using Winston, we’ll use logger.info to log the message we’ve extracted from the request body.

event_logger.js:
const express = require('express')
const router = express.Router()
const logger = require('../../utilities/backend_logger') router.post('/log', async (req, res) => { // Destructure request body sent by client const { severity, data } = req.body // Send response status of 200 back to client res.sendStatus(200) // send log messages to back-end logger logger.info(JSON.stringify({ severity, data }))
}) module.exports = router

Same as before, we can use Postman to test that this is working as expected. Send that same POST request from above. In Postman, you should see a response of “OK” because you’re sending back a response status of 200.

If you’re in development, you should also see a message logged to the browser console.

Step 3: Creating the Frontend Logger

I discovered that Winston is difficult to use in a Vue.js front-end due to creating too many polyfills. As a result, you need a separate front-end logger, and a way for it to talk to the back-end.

For the client-side logger, we’ll use vue-logger-plugin. I like this library because it allows you to create custom hooks. In this case, we’ll build a custom after hook that leverages another library, axios, to send log messages to your API endpoint.

Axios is a popular JavaScript library that allows you to send HTTP requests from a web browser or Node.js server. It provides an easy way to send asynchronous requests to the server, which means that the client code can continue running while the request is being sent and a response is being awaited. This is important for log messages, which are often sent in the background and shouldn't interrupt the main application logic.

frontend_logger.js:
import VueLogger from 'vue-logger-plugin'
import axios from 'axios' // after hook to send log messages to api endpoint
const ServerLogHook = { run: (event) => { axios.post('/api/event_logger/log', { severity: event.level, data: event.argumentArray }) }
} // define options
const options = { enabled: true, level: 'debug', afterHooks: [ServerLogHook]
} // export logger with applied options
export default new VueLogger(options)

Next, we’ll make our new front-end logger available for use by attaching it to our application.

main.js:
import Vue from 'vue';
import App from './App.vue'; // import front-end logging
import vueLogger from './utilities/frontend_logger' // for using front-end logger
Vue.use(vueLogger) new Vue({ render: (h) => h(App)
}).$mount('#app');

After it’s attached, the $log and $logger properties will be available globally in your Vue app. An easy way to test is to input this.$log or this.$logger directly into your Vue instance, so you’ll see these test messages when your Vue app loads.

main.js:
new Vue({ created: function() { const testObject = { name: 'test', value: 'this is a test object' } // using $log this.$log.debug('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.info('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.warn('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.error('Test Message', testObject) this.$log.log('Test Message', testObject) // using $logger this.$logger.debug('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.info('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.warn('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.error('Test Message', testObject) this.$logger.log('Test Message', testObject) }
})

Step 4: Hooking it all together

Now that all the pieces are in place, let’s try this out. Fire up  your application in development. You should see the test log messages appear in the console and in the `error.log` file, which we set in the Winston logger. You’re now free to add logging statements anywhere you please in your application on the frontend with `this.$log` and `this.$logger` and on the backend by including and invoking the Winston logger.

Conclusion

In summary, you can create a full stack logging solution by creating an API endpoint that passes log messages from a front-end logger to a back-end logger. The back-end logger then saves log messages to a file and optionally prints to the console. For a Vue.js and Node.js application, I used vue-logger-plugin for the front-end and Winston for the back-end. Use Postman to test your API endpoint along the way.

After the full stack logger is set up, you can customize according to your needs. The $log or $logger global properties can be used anywhere in your Vue app, including child components. With Winston, you can create additional custom transports to save information to your storage place of choice, such as a database.

Try this out, and please leave a comment of what you create if you do!

Items.Title: 学习 Vue.js：React 开发人员的新代码库之旅 | Learning Vue.js: A React Developer's Journey Through a New Codebase
Items.Description:
作为一名使用过 React 的 Web 开发人员，加入 Amplify 团队让我进入了 Vue.js 的世界。 在这篇文章中，我将讨论我学习 Vue.js 的经历以及我从 React 过渡时面临的挑战。

设置 Vue.js 的开发环境包括安装 Node.js、npm 以及使用 Vue CLI 创建和管理项目。 我熟悉了组件、指令和生命周期挂钩等核心概念。 为了有效地使用 Amplify 的代码库，我了解了用于状态管理和路由的 Vue.js 对应物：Vuex 和 Vue Router。

导航新代码库：从 React 到 Vue

从 React 过渡到 Vue.js 需要适应新的语法、结构和约定。 主要区别包括基于模板的语法、Vue.js 指令和单文件组件。 尽管存在这些差异，但基于组件的架构和道具等相似之处简化了过渡。
在 Amplify 的代码库上工作时，我面临着理解现有结构、实现功能和修复问题等挑战。 克服这些障碍教会了我宝贵的经验教训和使用 Vue.js 的最佳实践。

通过配置路线和添加导航链接，开发人员创建用户友好的界面。 路由参数和嵌套路由等功能可以帮助管理复杂的路由方案。 了解这些概念有助于开发人员更有效地使用 Vue.js 代码库。

用于状态管理的 Vuex

高效的状态管理对于 Web 应用程序至关重要，而 Vuex 是 Vue.js 的首选解决方案。 通过学习 Vuex 的运作方式，我深入了解了 Vue.js 应用程序中的状态管理，使我能够更有效地使用 Amplify 的代码库。

结论

我的 Vue.js 之旅充满了学习经验、挑战和宝贵的见解。 作为一名 React 开发人员，在处理 Amplify 的代码库时适应 Vue.js 最初是一项挑战。 然而，我发现 Vue.js 为构建 Web 应用程序提供了一个强大、灵活且易于使用的框架。

我鼓励您探索 Vue.js 并利用它的优势。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，Vue.js 都能提供很多帮助。 分享您的经验，为 Vue.js 社区做出贡献，并访问官方文档、课程、教程和讨论等资源。 编码愉快！



原文:

As a web developer experienced with React, joining the Amplify team introduced me to the world of Vue.js. Amplify is an open-source app empowering users to fight climate change by selecting climate campaigns and representatives to support. In this post, I'll discuss my experience learning Vue.js and the challenges I faced transitioning from React.

Vue.js Environment and Ecosystem

Setting up the development environment for Vue.js involves installing Node.js, npm, and using Vue CLI to create and manage projects. I familiarized myself with core concepts like components, directives, and lifecycle hooks. To effectively work with Amplify's codebase, I learned about Vue.js counterparts for state management and routing: Vuex and Vue Router.

Navigating a New Codebase: From React to Vue

Transitioning from React to Vue.js required adapting to new syntax, structure, and conventions. Key differences include template-based syntax, Vue.js directives, and single-file components. Despite these differences, similarities like component-based architecture and props eased the transition.
Working on Amplify's codebase, I faced challenges like understanding existing structure, implementing features, and fixing issues. Overcoming these hurdles taught me valuable lessons and best practices for working with Vue.js.

Vue Router and Navigation

Vue Router is essential for managing in-app navigation. By configuring routes and adding navigation links, developers create user-friendly interfaces. Features like route parameters and nested routes can help manage complex routing scenarios. Understanding these concepts helps developers work more effectively with Vue.js codebases.

Vuex for State Management

Efficient state management is crucial for web applications, and Vuex is the go-to solution for Vue.js. Vuex revolves around four main concepts: state, actions, mutations, and getters. By learning how Vuex operates, I gained insights into state management in Vue.js applications, allowing me to work more effectively with Amplify's codebase.

Conclusion

My journey into Vue.js was filled with learning experiences, challenges, and valuable insights. As a React developer, adapting to Vue.js while working on Amplify's codebase was initially challenging. However, I discovered that Vue.js offers a powerful, flexible, and approachable framework for building web applications.

I encourage you to explore Vue.js and take advantage of its benefits. Whether you're a beginner or experienced developer, Vue.js has much to offer. Share your experiences, contribute to the Vue.js community, and access resources like official documentation, courses, tutorials, and discussions. Happy coding!

Items.Title: 托管服务的演变格局：2023 年值得关注的趋势 | The Evolving Landscape of Managed Services: Trends to Watch in 2023
Items.Description:
随着技术继续快速发展，各行各业的企业越来越多地转向托管服务以增强其运营。 托管服务为公司提供了外包 IT 基础架构和支持的机会，使他们能够专注于自己的核心竞争力和战略目标。 到 2023 年，在有望彻底改变企业管理其技术需求的方式的新兴趋势的推动下，托管服务的格局将进一步发展。

云计算已经改变了企业存储、管理和访问数据的方式，这一趋势将在 2023 年继续。托管服务提供商将专注于提供全面的云解决方案，使企业能够扩展运营、提高敏捷性并增强安全。

此外，遵守《通用数据保护条例》(GDPR) 和《加州消费者隐私法》(CCPA) 等数据保护法规对企业来说至关重要，而托管服务提供商将在确保合规性方面发挥重要作用。

自动化将简化软件更新和系统维护等日常任务，释放 IT 资源以专注于更具战略意义的计划。

边缘**计算：
托管服务提供商将提供边缘计算解决方案，使企业能够利用物联网的力量，为客户提供更快、响应更快的服务。

托管服务提供商将在使企业能够通过提供可靠且响应迅速的 IT 服务来增强客户体验方面发挥关键作用。 这包括主动监控、快速解决问题和个性化支持，确保企业能够满足客户期望并保持竞争优势。

总之，在技术的快速进步和不断变化的业务需求的推动下，托管服务格局将在 2023 年发生重大变化。 云服务、安全性和合规性、人工智能和自动化、边缘计算、混合和多云环境、DevOps 和敏捷方法以及增强的客户体验将是值得关注的主要趋势。 通过拥抱这些趋势并与合适的托管服务提供商合作，企业可以定位自己在不断变化的数字环境中取得成功。



原文:

As technology continues to advance at a rapid pace, businesses across various industries are increasingly turning to managed services to enhance their operations. Managed services offer companies the opportunity to outsource IT infrastructure and support, allowing them to focus on their core competencies and strategic goals. In 2023, the landscape of managed services is set to evolve further, driven by emerging trends that promise to revolutionize the way businesses manage their technology needs.

**Cloud Services Dominance:
**One of the key trends in the managed services space is the continued dominance of cloud services. Cloud computing has already transformed the way businesses store, manage, and access their data, and this trend is set to continue in 2023. Managed service providers will focus on providing comprehensive cloud solutions that enable businesses to scale their operations, improve agility, and enhance security.

**Security and Compliance:
**With the increasing frequency and sophistication of cyber threats, security and compliance will be critical considerations for businesses in 2023. Managed service providers will need to offer robust security measures, including proactive monitoring, threat intelligence, and incident response. Additionally, compliance with data protection regulations, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), will be paramount for businesses, and managed service providers will play a vital role in ensuring compliance.

**Artificial Intelligence and Automation:
**Artificial intelligence (AI) and automation technologies will continue to reshape the managed services landscape in 2023. AI-powered tools can analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and proactively address potential issues, minimizing downtime and optimizing performance. Automation will streamline routine tasks, such as software updates and system maintenance, freeing up IT resources to focus on more strategic initiatives.

Edge **Computing:
**As the Internet of Things (IoT) expands and generates massive amounts of data, edge computing will gain prominence in 2023. Edge computing involves processing and analyzing data closer to its source, reducing latency and improving real-time decision-making capabilities. Managed service providers will offer edge computing solutions, enabling businesses to harness the power of IoT and deliver faster, more responsive services to their customers.

**Hybrid and Multi-Cloud Environments:
**Many businesses today operate in hybrid or multi-cloud environments, combining public and private cloud infrastructure to meet their specific needs. In 2023, managed service providers will focus on simplifying the management of these complex environments, providing seamless integration, efficient workload distribution, and unified monitoring and support across different cloud platforms.

**DevOps and Agile Methodologies:
**The adoption of DevOps practices and agile methodologies will continue to gain momentum in 2023. Managed service providers will help businesses embrace these approaches by providing the necessary tools and expertise to facilitate collaboration, streamline software development, and accelerate time-to-market. DevOps and agile methodologies enable organizations to respond quickly to changing market demands and deliver value to their customers more efficiently.

**Enhanced Customer Experience:
**In an increasingly competitive business landscape, delivering an exceptional customer experience will be a top priority for organizations. Managed service providers will play a crucial role in enabling businesses to enhance customer experience by providing reliable and responsive IT services. This includes proactive monitoring, rapid issue resolution, and personalized support, ensuring that businesses can meet customer expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

In conclusion, the managed services landscape is set to evolve significantly in 2023, driven by the rapid advancements in technology and changing business requirements. Cloud services, security and compliance, AI and automation, edge computing, hybrid and multi-cloud environments, DevOps and agile methodologies, and enhanced customer experience will be the key trends to watch. By embracing these trends and partnering with the right managed service providers, businesses can position themselves for success in the ever-changing digital landscape.

Items.Title: Windows 与 Linux | Windows vs Linux
Items.Description:
与 Windows 相比，软件和硬件的兼容性有限。 考虑到商品和兼容性的价值，很可能会影响到 Windows。 如题，querido lector，estás listo para tomar tu decisión en este gran duelo de los sistemas operativos？



原文:


Primero, el contendiente de Redmond, Washington: Windows. El querido niño mimado del mundo de los sistemas operativos, al menos en términos de usuarios de PC de escritorio. Su interfaz de usuario brillante y familiar ha conquistado los corazones (y los escritorios) de mucha gente alrededor del mundo. Su gran ventaja es la compatibilidad y facilidad de uso. Puedes instalar casi cualquier cosa en Windows. ¡Si tiene un microchip y una conexión USB, hay un 99% de probabilidades de que puedas conectarlo a una máquina con Windows! Y luego, está el universo de videojuegos, donde Windows tiene una clara ventaja con su amplia variedad de títulos.

Pero, no te dejes engañar por su apariencia amigable. Windows puede ser un poco exigente a veces. No estamos hablando de diva de Hollywood, pero sin duda necesita un poco más de mantenimiento. Actualizaciones que parecen tomar una eternidad, amenazas de seguridad ocasionales... ya sabes, las típicas cosas de Windows.

Ahora, veamos a nuestro segundo contendiente, el guerrero silencioso: Linux. Linux es como ese héroe de acción de las películas, rudo por fuera, pero con un corazón de oro por dentro. ¿Por qué? Bueno, para empezar, Linux es de código abierto, lo que significa que cualquiera puede ver, modificar y mejorar su código. ¡Habla de democracia en acción!

Linux es amado por los desarrolladores y los aficionados a la tecnología por su flexibilidad, seguridad y rendimiento. Y no te preocupes si piensas que Linux es solo para nerds de computadoras. Hay muchas distribuciones de Linux (piensa en ellas como "sabores" de Linux) que son tan fáciles de usar como Windows. Ubuntu, por ejemplo, es bastante amigable para el usuario.

Pero claro, Linux no es perfecto. La compatibilidad de software y hardware puede ser un poco más limitada en comparación con Windows. Si bien existen alternativas de código abierto para casi cualquier software que puedas necesitar, es posible que encuentres problemas si necesitas usar un software específico que solo se encuentra en Windows.

En resumen, ¿quién gana en el enfrentamiento Windows vs. Linux? Como en muchos aspectos de la vida, depende de tus necesidades y preferencias. Si valoras la comodidad y la compatibilidad sobre todo lo demás, probablemente te inclines hacia Windows. Pero si valoras la flexibilidad, la seguridad y el espíritu de código abierto, es posible que encuentres un nuevo mejor amigo en Linux.

Así que, querido lector, ¿estás listo para tomar tu decisión en este gran duelo de los sistemas operativos?

Items.Title: 如何在牢记用户的同时将 AASM 迁移到 ENUM | How to migrate AASM to ENUM while keeping users in mind
Items.Description:
在本文中，我们将解决从 AASM 状态机迁移到 Rails 内置枚举时的常见挑战。 我们旨在解决的主要问题是确保无缝过渡，而不会导致用户中断或数据不一致。 为了解决这个问题，我们将遵循系统的方法并将迁移过程分成两个拉取请求。 通过谨慎实施这些更改，我们可以确保平稳过渡，同时保持数据完整性。 让我们详细探讨解决方案。

为什么迁移到枚举？
作为我们团队与 Rails 约定保持一致的承诺的一部分，我们决定将以前使用 AASM 的模型迁移到枚举。 从 AASM 迁移到 Rails 枚举功能带来了一些好处，例如简化代码维护和通过优化数据库查询提高性能。 通过利用本机枚举功能，开发人员可以增强代码可读性，遵守 Rails 约定，并利用可用于在 Rails 中使用枚举的广泛社区支持。

这就是 aasm 状态机对于post模型的样子：

 include AASM aasm column: :aasm_state, whiny_transitions: false do state :draft, default: true state :published, enter: [:actions_on_publish] state :archived, enter: [:actions_on_archive] event :publish do transitions from: :draft, to: :published end event :archive do transitions from: :published, to: :archived end event :unarchive do transitions from: :archived, to: :published end end def actions_on_publish ### actions to perform when publishing a post end def actions_on_archive ### actions to perform when archiving a post end

挑战
当我们在不考虑现有数据和用户体验的情况下部署代码时，就会出现主要挑战。 如果我们直接切换到新的枚举状态列，用户会遇到意想不到的行为。 例如，在迁移完成之前，用户不会在帖子索引页面上看到他们的旧帖子。 发生这种情况是因为索引页将依赖于最初缺少迁移数据的新状态列。 同样的问题适用于访问职位数据的招聘人员。


建议的解决方案
为了解决这个问题，我们可以将迁移过程分为两个拉取请求 (PR)，并利用 rake 任务来促进数据迁移。 通过遵循这种方法，我们可以确保无缝过渡，而不会给用户带来不便。


第 1 步：PR #1
在这个初始 PR 中，我们将实施以下更改：

  1. 创建迁移：AddStateToPosts。

     class AddStateToPosts < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0] def change add_column :posts, :state, :string end end

  2. 在 Post 模型中实现一个 sync_state 方法，以保持枚举状态与现有 aasm_state 同步，直到我们在 PR#2 中删除 aasm_state。 还将该方法添加为 before_validation。

     class Post < ApplicationRecord before_validation :sync_state // AASM code private def sync_state self.state = aasm_state end end

  3. 开发一个 rake 任务 (single_run.rake) 以将现有的 aasm_state 值迁移到新的状态列。

     desc "2023-05-28: Migrate post aasm state to enum state" task migrate_post_aasm_state_to_enum_state: :environment do Post.all.distinct.pluck(:aasm_state).each do |state| posts = Post.where(aasm_state: state, state: nil) puts "Starting to migrate #{posts.count} #{state} posts" posts.in_batches do |batch| batch.update_all(state: state) end end puts "🏁 All done! 🏁" end

此时您还可以添加一个测试来验证枚举状态是否在每次更新 Post 的 aasm_state 时正确同步。

合并 PR 后运行以下代码：

rake "single_run:migrate_post_aasm_state_to_enum_state"

第 2 步：PR #2
在后续的 PR 中，我们可以进行以下更改：

  1. 从 Post 模型中删除 AASM 相关代码。

  2. 将枚举状态添加到 Post 模型，映射 AASM 状态。

     enum state: { draft: "draft", published: "published", archived: "archived", }, _default: "draft"

  3. 重写状态转换期间触发的操作以利用基于枚举的方法。

     def publish return if published? published! ### perform additional actions when publishing a post end def archive archived! ### perform additional actions when archiving a post end def unarchive published! if archived? ### perform additional actions when unarchiving a post end

最后但同样重要的是，检查在实例上调用 aasm_state 的代码，并将其替换为新的枚举状态。

例如：
posts.where.not(aasm_state: "draft")

现在将变成：
posts.where.not(state: "draft")

如果您希望新的实例方法继续工作，还可以查看旧操作在何处被调用（！）。

在帖子的控制器中，您可能有类似的内容：

 def archive resource.archive! end

现在将变成：

 def archive resource.archive end

当然你也可以做resource.archived! 但这只会改变状态而不会调用实例方法archive 。

就是这样，我们完成了🎉

结论：
通过遵循这种两步走的方法并使用 rake 任务进行数据迁移，我们可以从 AASM 状态机顺利过渡到 Rails 的内置枚举，而不会给用户带来问题或停机时间。

在此类迁移过程中考虑现有数据并保持一致的用户体验至关重要。 通过遵守 Rails 约定并实施建议的解决方案，我们确保了代码的一致性并提高了应用程序的可维护性。

感谢您阅读整篇文章，这对我来说意义重大。 这是第一个，希望更多。



原文:

In this article, we will address a common challenge when migrating from the AASM state machine to the Rails built-in enum. The main problem we aim to solve is ensuring a seamless transition without causing disruptions or data inconsistencies for users. To tackle this issue, we will follow a systematic approach and split the migration process into two pull requests. By carefully implementing these changes, we can ensure a smooth transition while maintaining data integrity. Let's explore the solution in detail.

Why Migrate to Enum?
As part of our teams commitment to align with Rails conventions, we have decided to migrate models that previously utilized AASM to enum. Migrating from AASM to the Rails enum feature brings benefits such as simplified code maintenance and improved performance through optimized database queries. By leveraging the native enum functionality, developers can enhance code readability, adhere to Rails conventions, and tap into the extensive community support available for working with enums in Rails.

This is how a aasm state machine might look like for a post model:

include AASM aasm column: :aasm_state, whiny_transitions: false do state :draft, default: true state :published, enter: [:actions_on_publish] state :archived, enter: [:actions_on_archive] event :publish do transitions from: :draft, to: :published end event :archive do transitions from: :published, to: :archived end event :unarchive do transitions from: :archived, to: :published end
end def actions_on_publish ### actions to perform when publishing a post
end def actions_on_archive ### actions to perform when archiving a post
end

The Challenge
The main challenge arises when we deploy the code without considering the existing data and user experience. If we were to directly switch to the new enum state column, users would encounter unexpected behavior. For instance, until the migration is completed, users would not see their old posts on the post index page. This occurs because the index page would rely on the new state column, which initially lacks the migrated data. The same issue applies to recruiters accessing the post data.

 
Proposed Solution
To tackle this problem, we can divide the migration process into two pull requests (PRs) and leverage a rake task to facilitate the data migration. By following this approach, we can ensure a seamless transition without causing inconvenience to users.

 
Step 1: PR #1
In this initial PR, we will implement the following changes:

  1. Create a migration: AddStateToPosts.

    class AddStateToPosts < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0] def change add_column :posts, :state, :string end
end

  2. Implement a sync_state method in the Post model to keep the enum state synchronized with the existing aasm_state until we remove the aasm_state in PR#2. Also add the method as a before_validation.

    class Post < ApplicationRecord before_validation :sync_state // AASM code private def sync_state self.state = aasm_state end
end

  3. Develop a rake task (single_run.rake) to migrate existing aasm_state values to the new state column.

    desc "2023-05-28: Migrate post aasm state to enum state"
task migrate_post_aasm_state_to_enum_state: :environment do Post.all.distinct.pluck(:aasm_state).each do |state| posts = Post.where(aasm_state: state, state: nil) puts "Starting to migrate #{posts.count} #{state} posts" posts.in_batches do |batch| batch.update_all(state: state) end end puts "🏁 All done! 🏁"
end

At this point you can also add a test to validate that the enum state is synced properly every time a Post's aasm_state is updated.

Once the PR is merged run following code:

rake "single_run:migrate_post_aasm_state_to_enum_state"
 

Step 2: PR #2
In this subsequent PR, we can proceed with the following changes:

  1. Remove the AASM-related code from the Post model.

  2. Add the enum state to the Post model, mapping the AASM states.

    enum state: { draft: "draft", published: "published", archived: "archived",
}, _default: "draft"

  3. Rewrite the actions triggered during state transitions to utilize the enum-based approach.

    def publish return if published? published! ### perform additional actions when publishing a post
end def archive archived! ### perform additional actions when archiving a post
end def unarchive published! if archived? ### perform additional actions when unarchiving a post
end

Last but not least, check in your code where the aasm_state was being called on an instance and swap it out with the new enum state.

For example:
posts.where.not(aasm_state: "draft")

will now become:
posts.where.not(state: "draft")

Also look out where the old actions where being called with a bang (!) if you want the new instance methods to work anymore.

In a controller for the posts you may had something like:

 def archive resource.archive! end

which will now become:

 def archive resource.archive end

Of course you could also do resource.archived! but that would only change the state and not call the instance method archive.

And that's it, we are done 🎉

Conclusion:
By following this two-step approach and employing a rake task for data migration, we can smoothly transition from the AASM state machine to Rails' built-in enum without causing problems or downtime for users.

It is crucial to consider existing data and maintain a consistent user experience during such migrations. By adhering to Rails conventions and implementing the proposed solution, we ensure code consistency and improve the maintainability of our application.

Thank you for reading the whole article, it means a lot to me. This is the first of hopefully more to come.
